title: Remote help: HTTPS screen-share guard (dbg153)
description:
Customer-initiated remote help no longer throws a raw JavaScript error when screen sharing is unavailable. Browsers only expose getDisplayMedia in a secure context (HTTPS or localhost), so customers on http://40.160.72.39/client-portal/ could not share their screen after an admin accepted.

The portal now checks navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia before calling it, warns early when Remote help is clicked over HTTP, shows themed chat system messages (HTTPS required or share cancelled), and ends the remote session cleanly instead of leaving WebRTC stuck at Connecting. Admins see a clear status in chat and the remote panel when the customer cannot share.

HTTPS note: OVH is configured for Let's Encrypt/nginx TLS (see deploy/ovh/nginx-horizon.conf), but the server is often accessed by raw IP over HTTP until DNS/TLS is in place. Remote screen share requires customers to use https://… on that host (or localhost for dev).

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare support remote help on the OVH private server. Deploy client-portal static assets (dbg153). No backend code change required for this fix. SaaS updates only via releases.html.
changeLog: Guard getDisplayMedia — no raw JS error on HTTP; friendly HTTPS system message in chat.
Early warning when customer clicks Remote help without a secure context.
Screen share cancelled/denied shows "Screen share was cancelled." and ends session cleanly.
Admin remote panel + chat message when customer cannot share (HTTPS required).
Cache bust dbg153 (portal runtime + PipeShare manifest).
